name: PV array design (deep) — DC/AC ratio, soiling, degradation, shading, bifacial
standard: IEC 61215-1:2021 (PV module design qualification) + IEC 61730-1/-2:2023 (PV module safety) + IEC 62548:2023 (PV array design) + IEC 60364-7-712:2017 (Electrical installations — PV) + IEC 61853 series (PV energy rating) + NREL Atmospheric Soiling Loss Method
description: Deep rule pack for PV array engineering. Validates module-level qualification (IEC 61215 design + IEC 61730 safety + fire-class C minimum for utility-scale, application class A — accessible mains-connected), array-level design per IEC 62548 (string voltage at lowest expected ambient < module Vmax_sys = 1500 V typical; overcurrent protection per IEC 60364-7-712; DC isolation; SPDs), system-level design (DC/AC ratio in 1.2 – 1.4 band for fixed-tilt utility-scale; inverter clipping loss budget < 1.5 % at chosen ratio; soiling-loss assumption documented with method — NREL/Kimber model or site-specific monitoring; degradation rate ≤ 0.5 %/yr crystalline LID+LeTID-corrected, ≤ 0.7 %/yr thin-film), shading analysis (PVsyst / Helioscope / SAM model, near + far shading, 3D obstruction model), bifacial gain factor (BGF) documented if bifacial modules (typical 5–15 % gain depending on albedo and structure), tracker design if single-axis (backtracking algorithm, max tilt ±60°, stow angle for high wind).
